In this Part, the following definition shall apply:

sections.chapter_title: General Governance for Small Non-Directive Insurers

rules.summary: This Chapter only applies to a small non-directive insurer.
A firm must take reasonable care to establish and maintain such systems and controls as are appropriate to its business.
A firm must take reasonable steps to establish and maintain adequate internal controls.
A firm must have in place an effective risk-management system.
A firm’s risk-management system must ensure that the __firm’s governing body is furnished with the information it needs to contribute to identifying, measuring, managing and controlling risks wh
A firm must establish and maintain appropriate systems and controls for managing operational risk that can arise from inadequacies or failures in its processes and systems (and, as appropriate, th
A firm must establish and maintain appropriate systems and controls for the management of its IT system risks.
A firm must establish and maintain appropriate systems and controls for the management of the risks involved in expected changes.
A firm must provide for an effective internal audit function.
The internal audit function must monitor the appropriateness and effectiveness of the __firm’s systems and controls.
The internal audit function must provide independent assurance to the firm’s governing body, audit committee or an appropriate senior manager of the integrity and effectiveness of the firm’s sys

sections.chapter_title: General Governance for Large Non-Directive Insurers

rules.summary: This Chapter only applies to a large non-directive insurer and a Swiss general insurer.
(1) A firm must have in place an effective system of governance which provides for sound and prudent management of its business.
A firm’s system of governance must be proportionate to the nature, scale and complexity of its operations.
A firm must:
The written policy on risk management referred to in 3.4(1) must comprise at least the specific policies required
A firm must take reasonable steps to ensure continuity and regularity in the performance of its activities, including the development of contingency plans, to which end, the firm must employ appro
A firm (other than a Swiss general insurer) must engage a broad set of qualities and competences when recruiting members to the governing body.
A firm must put in place a policy promoting diversity on the governing body.
A firm that maintains a website must explain on the website how it complies with the requirements of 3.7 and [3.
This Chapter only applies to a non-directive insurer that is also a non-directive friendly society.

A firm must take steps to obtain sufficient information from its outsourced provider to enable it to assess the impact of outsourcing on its systems and controls.
